methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $66M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
Chef Robotics is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company founded by Rajat Bhageria that has raised $65.6M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 private companies and 1 public company in the robotics and manufacturing sector, primarily at Series B stage, including 1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Collaborative Robotics, and Fairmat.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a valuation proxy, applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for advancement from Series A to subsequent rounds, yielding $131M. Peer-median analysis examines six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with valuations ranging from $300M to $10.0B; the median valuation across this set is $1.1B. This wide dispersion reflects material heterogeneity in company maturity and market position within the robotics sector.

The two methods produce a triangulated range of $112M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $603M. The stage-progression method anchors the lower bound, while the peer-median approach defines the upper boundary. Key uncertainties include the appropriate adjustment for stage discount relative to Series B comparables, which span nearly 34x in valuation, and the applicability of peer multiples to a pre-Series B company with differentiated capabilities. A valuation in the $200M–$400M range may better reflect Chef Robotics' stage position relative to the broader peer distribution.
